XOnlineMessageSend

Sends a message to one or more players.

HRESULT XOnlineMessageSend(
    DWORD dwUserIndexSender,
    XONLINE_MSG_HANDLE hMsg,
    DWORD dwRecipientCount,
    const XUID *pxuidRecipients,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE phTask
);

Parameters

dwUserIndexSender
[in] Index number of the sender.
hMsg
[in] Handle of the message to send.
dwRecipientCount
[in] Number of recipients for the message.
pxuidRecipients
[in] Array of player IDs. The number of IDs in this array must match the value in dwRecipientCount.
hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle of the event you want triggered every time there is work to perform on this task. If you do not want to assign an event, set this parameter to NULL.
phTask
[out] Pointer to an XONLINETASK_HANDLE type. The handle is associated with the task started by this function.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

Titles send most types of messages using other functions, such as XOnlineGameInviteSend. Generally, only messages of type XONLINE_MSG_TYPE_TITLE_CUSTOM are sent using XOnlineMessageSend.

The handle your title receives through the phTask parameter should not be released until the send operation completes. Also, your title should not destroy the message object it receives through the hMsg parameter until the send operation is done.

When sending a message using a family title ID, titles must also specify that family title ID to the XOnlineMessageEnableReceivingFamilyTitleIDs function. This step is necessary in order to receive notifications of the incoming message through the XOnlineGetNotificationEx function or to have the message returned by the XOnlineMessageEnumerate function. For more information on family title IDs, see the XOnlineMessageSetSendingFamilyTitleID function.

Adding directory attachments to a message requires additional memory to be allocated from the private heap (allocated when the XOnlineStartup function is called). This allocation can cause the XOnlineMessageSend function operation to fail with the value E_OUTOFMEMORY. To avoid this, make the XOnlineMessageSetProperty call to add the directory attachment the last call after adding all other properties. If you are adding more than one directory attachment, increase the default private heap size by about 45 KB; that is, pass to XOnlineStartup a value of 177152 for the dwMaxPrivatePool member of the XONLINE_STARTUP_PARAMS structure.

The total size limit for all message data is 64 KB. The Xbox Live service adds some overhead, so you should plan on a usable limit of about 62 KB. You can allocate space up to this limit in any way you see fit. For instance, you can have one property that is 62 KB, two properties that are 50 KB and 12 KB, or whatever else you need.

If the send operation completes successfully, the XOnlineTaskContinue function indicates task completion by returning the value XONLINETASK_S_SUCCESS. If delivery to one or more recipients fails, XOnlineTaskContinue returns S_FALSE.

To retrieve the message IDs assigned to successful send operations—or the error codes for failed send operations—call the XOnlineMessageSendGetResults function using the completed XOnlineMessageSend task handle.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.
